items.hook: Battle of Anzen: The Byzantine emperor Theophilos suffers a heavy defeat by the Abbasids.
items.body: The Battle of Anzen or Dazimon was fought on 22 July 838 at Anzen or Dazimon between the Byzantine Empire and the forces of the Abbasid Caliphate. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Heinz in the Moon is a 1934 German comedy film directed by Robert A.
items.body: Stemmle and starring Heinz Rühmann, Rudolf Platte and Annemarie Sörensen. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Little Brother review – Netflix comedy is neither weird or funny enough for star Eric André
items.body:
The surreal comedian struggles to sell a middling and mostly conventional film about an uptight realtor whose life is upturned by an unpredictable figure from his past

items.hook: The 100 best novels of all time
items.body: The top 100 novels of all time published in English, as voted for by authors, critics and academics worldwide. How many have you read?

items.body:

items.hook: Parrot and Olivier in America is a novel by Australian writer Peter Carey.
items.body: It was on the shortlist of six books for the 2010 Man Booker Prize. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Georgian organisations claim state festivals are being hijacked by “authoritarian regime”
items.body: Top Georgian groups say international guests are being used as “useful idiots”.

items.hook: The first ever motor race is held in France between the cities of Paris and Rouen. The fastest finisher was the Comte Jules-Albert de Dion, but the "official" victory was awarded to Albert Lemaître driving his three-horsepower petrol engined Peugeot.
items.body: Paris–Rouen was a pioneering city-to-city motoring competition in 1894 which is sometimes described as the world's first competitive motor race. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Rif War: The Spanish Army suffers its worst military defeat in modern times to the Berbers of the Rif region of Spanish Morocco.
items.body: The Rif War was an armed conflict fought from 1921 to 1926 between Spain and Berber tribes of the mountainous Rif region of northern Morocco. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Sugaring Time is a 1983 children's nonfiction book written by Kathryn Lasky with photographs from her husband Christopher G.
items.body: The story follows a Vermont family, the Laceys, as they diligently work to collect and process sap into syrup, beginning with tapping the trees to allow the sap to flow out; the sap is collected and brought to the sugar house for boiling, which evaporates the

items.hook: Spanish Civil War: The Popular Executive Committee of Valencia takes power in the Valencian Community.
items.body: The Spanish Civil War was fought from 1936 to 1939 between the Republicans and the Nationalist rebels. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Jean Charles de Menezes is killed by police as the hunt begins for the London Bombers responsible for the 7 July 2005 London bombings and the 21 July 2005 London bombings.
items.body: Jean Charles da Silva de Menezes was a Brazilian man fatally shot and wrongfully killed by the Metropolitan Police Service at Stockwell Station of the London Underground, after being mistakenly identified as one of the fugitives from the previous day's failed bombing attempts. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Charles Gaines, whose book Pumping Iron helped to popularize bodybuilding, dies at 84
items.body:
1974 book led to making of seminal documentary and introduced Arnold Schwarzenegger to the world

items.hook: EU approves Paramount’s takeover of Warner Bros Discovery with conditions
items.body: Paramount agrees to end United International Pictures (UIP) film distribution joint venture with Universal Pictures.

items.hook: Combat Zone: True Tales of GIs in Iraq is a graphic novel written by Karl Zinsmeister.
items.body: Penciller Dan Jurgens illustrated the graphic novel with cover art contributed by Esad Ribic. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Godzilla vs Kong actor Kaylee Hottle dies aged 18
items.body:
The deaf actor, who played a major role in Godzilla vs Kong and its 2024 sequel, was killed in a car crash in Maryland

items.hook: Colonel Newcome is a 1906 play by the British writer Michael Morton.
items.body: It is based on the character from the 1855 William Makepeace Thackeray novel The Newcomes. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Martial law in Poland is officially revoked.
items.body: Martial law in Poland existed between 13 December 1981 and 22 July 1983. The world is stranger than it lets on.
